diagrams_decision: APPROVED without new diagrams. Writer's judgment to drop legacy SVGs is sound: §4.5's 19-step verbatim demo replay (with admission/preemption/finish callouts) serves the same purpose as a timeline figure for budget allocation. §4.1's cafeteria analogy + §4.2's narrative running-first reasoning + §4.4's tables convey the abstract concepts. None of the abstract concepts (budget allocation, two-phase scheduling, preempt-tail rule, chunked-prefill on/off) read as confusing without a figure — each is grounded in either a code block, a small workload table, or a numbered list of decision points. Reviewer judgment: NOT a REJECT trigger.
novel_insights_verified: Bubble math uses actual demo workload (P=400/64/16, O=4/8/16) — formula T_static=400+16=416 ties to demo output 'static: 416 steps' exactly.
Three asserts framing as 'final correctness gate' (§4.2.1) is correctly tied to scheduler.py:L848-L853.
Phase 1 'continue' vs Phase 2 'break' semantic difference is the closing teaching point of §4.2.4 — accurate per scheduler.py:L446-L462.
Minimal preempt-trigger config (num_gpu_blocks=2) in §4.6 is reproducible.
F01 forward-pointer to Ch20 in §4.3.4 explains finished_req_ids gracefully without breaking flow.
All source paths use full {source_dir}/path:Lline form on first reference.
non_blocking_observations: Inline-density warnings on lines 92/118/150/171 — none REJECT-worthy because each inline is a single Greek letter or single subscript variable used in derivation. Promoting them to display blocks would actually hurt readability.
Front-matter line 8 has trailing Chinese parenthetical '（call sites only）' inside a backtick path — cosmetic only, doesn't affect grounding.

Section 4.7's table is 13 rows in chapter.md but the row counter only counted lines starting with '| ' globally (29 across all tables); confirmed visually that §4.7 table has 13 rows + 1 separator + 1 header = covers 13 distinct mappings, well over the ≥5 threshold.
